caveats: a: Do not substitute occupation evidence from portfolio managers, fiduciary agencies, or estate-administration firms classified outside 525920.
rho: Implementation evidence from trustees, law firms, banks, or investment advisers would describe service-provider industries rather than this legal-entity code.
e: The zero applies to the frozen operating-service lens, not to the economic value or legal significance of trusts and estates.
s5: Changes of trustee, beneficiary distributions, deaths, and trust restructurings are not qualifying control transfers of an operating service firm.
q: Trustee or adviser fees should not be imported from establishments classified in portfolio management or fiduciary services.
d5: Growth in trust assets, estate filings, or beneficiary counts is not equivalent to demand for an in-scope outsourced service basket.
o: Legal requirements for a fiduciary do not establish that the fiduciary service provider is classified in 525920.

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: The classified entities hold or administer assets under governing instruments for beneficiaries and earn property income; they do not have external customers buying services. Fee-based fiduciary and portfolio-management providers sit outside this code.
lens.excluded_activities: Trusts, estates, bankruptcy estates, and agency accounts administered for beneficiaries are excluded as non-operating legal vehicles. Portfolio managers, personal-estate administrators, and operating businesses held by trusts are excluded because Census classifies those establishments in other industries.
lens.included_activities: No operating population survives the default lens: this code contains the trusts, estates, and agency-account legal entities themselves, not firms selling recurring outsourced services.

caveats: Do not substitute occupation evidence from portfolio managers, fiduciary agencies, or estate-administration firms classified outside 525920.
a.change_evidence: A Census establishment microdata tabulation showing staffed 525920 establishments that sell recurring services to external customers, together with their occupation mix, would permit estimation.
a.rationale: Wage-weighted task exposure is undefined because no operating outsourced-service firm population remains under the lens, and Census reports that entities in this subsector have little or no employment.

caveats: Growth in trust assets, estate filings, or beneficiary counts is not equivalent to demand for an in-scope outsourced service basket.
d5.change_evidence: A product-level Census or administrative dataset demonstrating recurring service output from correctly classified 525920 establishments would define a measurable current basket and forecast base.
d5.rationale: A five-year ratio for the current service basket is undefined because no qualifying service basket exists inside the frozen lens.

caveats: The zero applies to the frozen operating-service lens, not to the economic value or legal significance of trusts and estates.
e.change_evidence: Only a revised Census classification or establishment-level evidence that 525920 includes firms with material recurring external service revenue would change this structural boundary.
e.rationale: Census defines 525920 as legal entities administered for beneficiaries and explicitly classifies portfolio management, personal-estate administration, and operating businesses elsewhere. Census also states that subsector 525 entities have no revenue from selling services. The share eligible under the outsourced-service-firm lens is therefore a structural zero.

l.detail: SUSB scope excludes this activity, no IPS labor-compensation/output series at any level, and no other receipts source downloaded; labor_share left null (recorded gap).
l.rationale: dataset labor_share has no defensible value

n.detail: margin 0.2102 (margins.json[52]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Financial Svcs. (Non-bank & Insurance) EBITDA/Sales 21.02%. Note: Damodaran reports 0.00% for banks (EBITDA not meaningful); bank codes inherit this non-bank default and are flagged ESTIMATE like all n_band values.); revenue/employee $605,324 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 5259); DATA GAP: no SUSB size-class data for this code; n_band could not be computed — the research run must supply it with a shown chain, quality ESTIMATE
n.rationale: dataset n_band has no defensible value

caveats: Legal requirements for a fiduciary do not establish that the fiduciary service provider is classified in 525920.
o.change_evidence: Evidence of a staffed, accountable 525920 operator selling recurring services, paired with customer workflow studies separating software self-service from operator delivery, would permit estimation.
o.rationale: Operator-required share is undefined because the frozen lens contains no qualifying operator or current service quantity.

caveats: Trustee or adviser fees should not be imported from establishments classified in portfolio management or fiduciary services.
q.change_evidence: Verified contracts and revenue records from staffed 525920 firms showing recurring third-party service sales and renewal pricing would provide the necessary billing evidence.
q.rationale: Commercial retention is undefined because the classified entities do not sell services to external customers under the frozen lens.

caveats: Implementation evidence from trustees, law firms, banks, or investment advisers would describe service-provider industries rather than this legal-entity code.
rho.change_evidence: Workflow audits from staffed 525920 entities that independently verify external service revenue and identify implementable AI use cases would establish a basis for this primitive.
rho.rationale: Five-year implementation cannot be estimated without an eligible operating firm population or a defined exposed task base.

caveats: Changes of trustee, beneficiary distributions, deaths, and trust restructurings are not qualifying control transfers of an operating service firm.
s5.change_evidence: A transaction database identifying control sales of independently operating 525920 service businesses, with NAICS verification and going-concern operations, would make this estimable.
s5.rationale: There are no eligible transferable operating firms in the frozen lens, so a control-transfer probability for such firms is not defined.

principal_driver: Census explicitly separates the beneficiary legal entities in 525920 from fee-based administration and portfolio-management providers.
principal_weakness: No eligible operating-service firm population remains under the frozen lens.

sources.supports: Defines 525920 as trusts, estates, or agency-account legal entities administered for beneficiaries; states subsector 525 entities earn property income, have little or no employment, and no service-sale revenue; and cross-references portfolio management, personal-estate administration, and operating businesses to other industries.
sources.title: NAICS Sector 52: Finance and Insurance

sources.supports: Describes a trust or decedent's estate as a separate legal entity for federal tax purposes and explains that a decedent's estate generally exists until final distribution of its assets.
sources.title: Instructions for Form 1041 and Schedules A, B, G, J, and K-1 (2025)

title: Trusts, Estates, and Agency Accounts
